Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Case
Off Calendar Apr-24-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jeffrey K. Brown, Esq. of Payne & Fears LLP, Tel. 949.797.1231, for defendant and moving party;
Maurice J. Fitzgerald, Esq. of The Cartwright Law Firm, Tel. 415.433.0444, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS HEALD COLLEGE LLC'S and CORINTHIAN COLLEGES, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Case is granted. Whether the Court relies on the December 15, 2008 or January 13, 2009 arbitration agreement, they both incorporate by reference JAMS Rule 11(c). JAMS Rule 11(c) provides that: "Jurisdictional and arbitrability disputes, including disputes over the formation, existence, validity, interpretation or scope of the agreement under which Arbitration is sought, and who are proper Parties to the Arbitration, shall be submitted to and ruled on by the Arbitrator. Unless the relevant law requires otherwise, the Arbitrator has the authority to determine jurisdiction and arbitrability issue as a preliminary matter." (Cute Declaration, para. 7, Ex. C.) Accordingly, the parties agreed to arbitrate arbitrability and the arbitrator will decide as a preliminary matter whether defendant waived the right to arbitrate and whether the promotion letter of September 9, 2009, supersedes the parties' agreement to arbitrate. See Oracle Am., Inc. v. Myriad Grp. A.G., 724 F.3d 1069, 1074 (9th Cir. 2013); Rodriguez v. Am. Technologies, Inc. (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 1110, 1123; Dream Theater, Inc. v. Dream Theater (2004) 124 Cal.App.4th 547, 557. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
